tables.name: Bramble Signs
rows: Thorns growing in a perfect circle
A rag caught on a branch, still damp
Silence where there should be birdsong
A path through the thicket that wasn't there this morning
Berries that are definitely not the color they should be
A cold patch of air with no source

tables.name: Bramble Dwellers
rows: A woodcutter who won't go past a certain tree
A trapper checking snares that are always empty
A child who says the bramble talks to her
An old hunting dog that refuses to enter, no matter what
A charcoal-burner who works only at the bramble's edge
Someone singing, just out of sight, just out of tune

sections.title: Setting
lines: The bramble has grown a little every year for as long as anyone remembers.
Nothing that goes in deep comes back out the way it went in.
Locals leave small offerings at the treeline out of habit, not belief.

scene.title: The Path That Wasn't There
scene.body: A gap in the bramble opens onto a path you're certain wasn't there yesterday. It's already getting dark.
